agent-foundry.description: Azure AI Foundry agent lifecycle management — scaffold, deploy, test, and manage AI agents with Azure AI Foundry MCP integration

actions.name: af-agent-status
actions.description: Show the status and run history of a deployed Azure AI Foundry agent -- displays agent configuration, recent runs, token usage, and health diagnostics

actions.name: af-deploy-agent
actions.description: Deploy an agent to Azure AI Foundry -- reads an agent JSON file or extracts the payload from a Claude Code .md file and creates or updates the agent via the MCP server

actions.name: af-list-agents
actions.description: List all agents deployed in the configured Azure AI Foundry project -- shows agent ID, name, model, description, and creation date in a formatted table

actions.name: af-scaffold-agent
actions.description: Scaffold a new AI agent from a natural language description -- invokes the Agent Generator to produce both a Claude Code .md file and an Azure AI Foundry agent JSON payload

actions.name: af-setup
actions.description: Set up the Agent Foundry plugin -- install prerequisites, configure Azure AI Foundry credentials, and verify the MCP server connection

actions.name: af-test-agent
actions.description: Test a deployed Azure AI Foundry agent by creating a thread, sending a message, and displaying the response -- supports interactive multi-turn conversations

prerequisites: `Azure AI Developer` — full agent lifecycle (create, run, read, delete)
`Azure AI User` — run agents and read results only (no create/delete)
`Cognitive Services OpenAI User` — if using Azure OpenAI endpoints directly

azure-document-intelligence.description: Azure AI Document Intelligence — OCR, prebuilt models (invoices, receipts, IDs, tax forms), custom models, layout analysis, document classification, and batch processing

actions.name: docai-analyze
actions.description: Analyze a document with a prebuilt or custom Document Intelligence model — extract fields, tables, and text from invoices, receipts, IDs, tax forms, or any document type

actions.name: docai-batch
actions.description: Batch analyze multiple documents with Document Intelligence — process entire Blob containers, monitor batch jobs, and retrieve results at scale

actions.name: docai-classify
actions.description: Build and use a Document Intelligence classifier — train a classifier to identify document types and split multi-document files

actions.name: docai-custom-model
actions.description: Build a custom Document Intelligence extraction model — prepare training data, label fields, train, evaluate accuracy, and deploy for production use

actions.name: docai-setup
actions.description: Set up the Azure Document Intelligence plugin — validate RBAC roles, provision or discover a Document Intelligence resource, check connectivity, and configure environment variables

prerequisites: Document Intelligence resource (S0 tier required for custom models)
Document Intelligence resource (S0 tier)
Document Intelligence resource configured (run `/docai-setup` first)
Environment variables: `DOCUMENT_INTELLIGENCE_ENDPOINT`, `DOCUMENT_INTELLIGENCE_KEY`
Result container in Azure Blob Storage (can be the same storage account)
Run `/docai-setup` to configure endpoint and keys
SAS tokens with appropriate permissions (Read+List for source, Write for results)
Source documents in Azure Blob Storage
Training data in Azure Blob Storage with labels
Training data organized by document type in Blob Storage

actions.description: Generate a FetchXML or OData query for Dataverse data retrieval

actions.description: Create a relationship (1:N or N:N) between Dataverse tables

actions.description: Seed data into a Dataverse table from CSV, JSON, or generated test data

actions.description: Set up the Dataverse Schema plugin — configure environment URL, auth credentials, publisher prefix, and verify Web API connectivity

actions.name: dataverse-solution-export
actions.description: Export a Dataverse solution as managed or unmanaged zip

actions.name: dataverse-solution-import
actions.description: Import a Dataverse solution zip into a target environment

actions.name: dataverse-table-create
actions.description: Create a new Dataverse custom table with columns via the Web API

prerequisites: **Aggregation** needs (count, sum, average, group by)
**Child/Referencing table** (the "many" side for 1:N, or Entity2 for N:N)
**Column display name** (e.g., "Estimated Hours")
**Column type** or a description of what the column stores (use the column type decision tree to determine the correct type)
**Data source**: One of:
**Description** of what the table stores
**Environment URL**
**Export type**: Managed or Unmanaged (default: both)
**Filter conditions** (status, date range, specific values, related record conditions)
**Global or Local**:
**Import options**:
**Initial columns** beyond the primary name column (optional, can be added later)
**Option set name** (display name, e.g., "Task Priority")
**Options**: List of choices with labels (values will be auto-assigned if not specified)
**Output directory** for the zip file
**Ownership type**: UserOwned (default) or OrganizationOwned
**Pagination** requirements (page size, specific page)
**Parent/Referenced table** (the "one" side for 1:N, or Entity1 for N:N)
**Preferred format**: FetchXML or OData (recommend FetchXML for complex queries, OData for simple ones)
**Publisher prefix** (e.g., `cr123`)
**Publisher prefix** (e.g., `cr123`) — ask if not obvious from context
**Related data** to include (join/expand other tables)
**Relationship type**: 1:N (One-to-Many) or N:N (Many-to-Many)
**Required level**: None, Recommended, or ApplicationRequired
**Script language**: TypeScript (default) or Bash/curl
**Solution unique name**
**Solution unique name** (e.g., `ContosoProjectManagement`)
**Solution unique name** — ask if not obvious from context
**Solution zip file path** (local path to the .zip file)
**Sorting** requirements
**Synchronous or Asynchronous**: Async recommended for production
**Table logical name** (e.g., `cr123_project`)
**Table name** (display name, e.g., "Project Task")
**Target environment URL** (e.g., `https://org-test.crm.dynamics.com`)
**Target table** logical name and entity set name
**What data** they want to retrieve (which table, which columns)

license-optimizer.description: M365 license optimization for MSPs/CSPs — identify inactive licenses, map downgrades and upgrades, estimate savings, and generate multi-tenant Lighthouse reports for customer review meetings

actions.name: license-report
actions.description: Generate a customer-facing license optimization report for review meetings — includes savings summary, recommendations, and multi-tenant Lighthouse comparison.

actions.name: license-scan
actions.description: Scan for inactive and underused licenses — identify waste, map downgrade candidates, and estimate monthly savings.

actions.name: license-setup
actions.description: Set up the License Optimizer plugin — configure Graph access for license reporting and usage analytics

prerequisites: Azure AD Premium P1 (required for `signInActivity` data)
For MSPs: Active GDAP relationships with License Administrator or Global Reader roles
Graph API access with `User.Read.All`, `Directory.Read.All`, `Reports.Read.All`

m365-admin.description: M365 tenant admin via Microsoft Graph — users, groups, licenses, Exchange, SharePoint, Teams, Intune, PIM, access reviews, usage reports, guest management, administrative units, Microsoft Search, and domain/federation management

actions.name: m365-admin-units
actions.description: Manage Administrative Units — create AUs, add members, assign scoped admin roles for delegated administration without tenant-wide permissions.

actions.name: m365-audit
actions.description: Generate M365 audit reports — sign-in logs, directory changes, license usage, permission audits. Export as markdown tables or CSV.

actions.name: m365-domain-management
actions.description: Manage tenant domains — add custom domains, retrieve DNS verification records, verify domains, set default domain, and manage SAML/WS-Fed federation.

actions.name: m365-exchange-mailbox
actions.description: Exchange Online mailbox operations — create shared mailbox, set auto-reply, manage delegates, convert user mailbox to shared.

actions.name: m365-group-create
actions.description: Create an M365 group — security group, M365 (Unified) group, or distribution list. Optionally add initial members from CSV.

actions.name: m365-guest-management
actions.description: Manage B2B guest users — invite guests, list stale guests, review cross-tenant access policies, and clean up inactive guest accounts.

actions.name: m365-intune-devices
actions.description: Intune device management — list devices, trigger device actions (wipe/retire/sync/lock), review compliance status, and manage compliance policies.

actions.name: m365-license-assign
actions.description: Assign, change, or revoke M365 licenses. Lists available SKUs, validates availability, and supports bulk operations from CSV.

actions.name: m365-offboard-wizard
actions.description: Guided employee offboarding checklist — mailbox forwarding, OneDrive transfer, license removal, session revocation, risky app cleanup, and access review. Supports Lighthouse multi-tenant batch mode.

actions.name: m365-onboard-wizard
actions.description: Guided "Set up new employee" wizard — create account, assign license, add to groups, provision Teams, set up OneDrive folder template, and send welcome email. Supports Lighthouse multi-tenant mode.

actions.name: m365-pim-roles
actions.description: PIM role management — make users eligible for directory roles, activate/deactivate roles JIT, manage access reviews, and report privileged access.

actions.name: m365-search-admin
actions.description: Manage Microsoft Search — create and update bookmarks, Q&As, and acronyms for organizational search results.

actions.name: m365-setup
actions.description: Set up the M365 Admin plugin — configure Azure app registration, install dependencies, and verify Graph API connectivity

actions.name: m365-sharepoint-site
actions.description: SharePoint site operations — create site, manage permissions, set sharing policy, associate with hub site.

actions.name: m365-teams-admin
actions.description: Administer Microsoft Teams — create/update/archive teams, manage channels, membership, apps, and messaging/meeting policies.

actions.name: m365-usage-reports
actions.description: Generate M365 usage and adoption reports — email, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint, mailbox usage, and active user counts across configurable time periods.

actions.name: m365-user-create
actions.description: Create M365 user(s) with license and group assignment. Supports single user from params or bulk from CSV. Includes validation and dry-run.

actions.name: m365-user-offboard
actions.description: Offboard M365 user(s) — disable account, revoke licenses, remove from groups, set auto-reply, convert mailbox to shared, transfer OneDrive. Supports bulk from CSV.

prerequisites: Directory: Users with admin roles, role assignments
SharePoint: Site permissions, external sharing links, guest access
`--action <list|grant|revoke>`: Permission action
`--include-external`: Include external sharing details
`--role <read|write|owner>`: Permission level
`--scope <directory|sharepoint|all>`: What to audit
`--site <siteUrl>`: Specific SharePoint site to audit (for sharepoint scope)
`--url <siteUrl>`: Target site URL
`--user <upn>`: User to grant/revoke

actions.name: m365-admin-units
actions.description: Manage Administrative Units — create AUs, add members, assign scoped admin roles for delegated administration without tenant-wide permissions.

actions.name: m365-audit
actions.description: Generate M365 audit reports — sign-in logs, directory changes, license usage, permission audits. Export as markdown tables or CSV.

actions.name: m365-domain-management
actions.description: Manage tenant domains — add custom domains, retrieve DNS verification records, verify domains, set default domain, and manage SAML/WS-Fed federation.

actions.name: m365-exchange-mailbox
actions.description: Exchange Online mailbox operations — create shared mailbox, set auto-reply, manage delegates, convert user mailbox to shared.

actions.name: m365-group-create
actions.description: Create an M365 group — security group, M365 (Unified) group, or distribution list. Optionally add initial members from CSV.

actions.name: m365-guest-management
actions.description: Manage B2B guest users — invite guests, list stale guests, review cross-tenant access policies, and clean up inactive guest accounts.

actions.name: m365-intune-devices
actions.description: Intune device management — list devices, trigger device actions (wipe/retire/sync/lock), review compliance status, and manage compliance policies.

actions.name: m365-license-assign
actions.description: Assign, change, or revoke M365 licenses. Lists available SKUs, validates availability, and supports bulk operations from CSV.

actions.name: m365-offboard-wizard
actions.description: Guided employee offboarding checklist — mailbox forwarding, OneDrive transfer, license removal, session revocation, risky app cleanup, and access review. Supports Lighthouse multi-tenant batch mode.

actions.name: m365-onboard-wizard
actions.description: Guided "Set up new employee" wizard — create account, assign license, add to groups, provision Teams, set up OneDrive folder template, and send welcome email. Supports Lighthouse multi-tenant mode.

actions.name: m365-pim-roles
actions.description: PIM role management — make users eligible for directory roles, activate/deactivate roles JIT, manage access reviews, and report privileged access.

actions.name: m365-search-admin
actions.description: Manage Microsoft Search — create and update bookmarks, Q&As, and acronyms for organizational search results.

actions.name: m365-setup
actions.description: Set up the M365 Admin plugin — configure Azure app registration, install dependencies, and verify Graph API connectivity

actions.name: m365-sharepoint-site
actions.description: SharePoint site operations — create site, manage permissions, set sharing policy, associate with hub site.

actions.name: m365-teams-admin
actions.description: Administer Microsoft Teams — create/update/archive teams, manage channels, membership, apps, and messaging/meeting policies.

actions.name: m365-usage-reports
actions.description: Generate M365 usage and adoption reports — email, Teams, OneDrive, SharePoint, mailbox usage, and active user counts across configurable time periods.

actions.name: m365-user-create
actions.description: Create M365 user(s) with license and group assignment. Supports single user from params or bulk from CSV. Includes validation and dry-run.

actions.name: m365-user-offboard
actions.description: Offboard M365 user(s) — disable account, revoke licenses, remove from groups, set auto-reply, convert mailbox to shared, transfer OneDrive. Supports bulk from CSV.

prerequisites: Directory: Users with admin roles, role assignments
SharePoint: Site permissions, external sharing links, guest access
`--action <list|grant|revoke>`: Permission action
`--include-external`: Include external sharing details
`--role <read|write|owner>`: Permission level
`--scope <directory|sharepoint|all>`: What to audit
`--site <siteUrl>`: Specific SharePoint site to audit (for sharepoint scope)
`--url <siteUrl>`: Target site URL
`--user <upn>`: User to grant/revoke

actions.name: m365-admin-units
actions.description: Manage Administrative Units — create AUs, add members, assign scoped admin roles for delegated administration without tenant-wide permissions.

actions.name: m365-audit
actions.description: Generate M365 audit reports — sign-in logs, directory changes, license usage, permission audits. Export as markdown tables or CSV.

actions.name: m365-domain-management
actions.description: Manage tenant domains — add custom domains, retrieve DNS verification records, verify domains, set default domain, and manage SAML/WS-Fed federation.

microsoft-azure-mcp.description: Inspect subscriptions, resource groups, and resources via MCP.

prerequisites: `https://management.azure.com/user_impersonation` - Access to Azure Resource Manager

msp-tenant-provisioning.description: Full MSP/CSP new customer provisioning — Partner Center CSP tenant creation, Azure subscription and management group setup, initial M365 security baseline, domain DNS configuration, and Microsoft 365 Lighthouse onboarding.

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-azure-setup
actions.description: Set up Azure infrastructure for a new customer — create or link an Azure subscription, place it in the management group hierarchy, assign initial RBAC (MSP team + customer owner), apply Azure Policy baseline (Security Benchmark, tag governance), create a budget with alerts, enable Defender for Cloud across all plans, and prepare for Lighthouse delegation.

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-configure
actions.description: Apply the initial Microsoft 365 security baseline to a newly provisioned tenant — create break-glass accounts, disable security defaults, deploy Conditional Access policies (MFA, block legacy auth, block high-risk sign-ins), configure PIM eligible role assignments, set authentication methods, enable audit logging, and verify the configuration.

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-domain-setup
actions.description: Add and verify a custom domain for a Microsoft 365 tenant — add the domain via Graph API, retrieve DNS verification TXT records, guide DNS record creation for M365 services (MX, autodiscover CNAME, SRV, SPF, DKIM, DMARC), verify the domain, set as primary, and update user UPNs.

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-lighthouse-onboard
actions.description: Complete the final onboarding of a new customer tenant into the MSP management plane — enroll the M365 tenant in Microsoft 365 Lighthouse, deploy the initial management template baseline, set up Azure Lighthouse delegation for Azure subscriptions, configure GDAP role assignments, verify all access paths, and generate a handover checklist for the customer.

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-provision
actions.description: Create a new Microsoft 365 tenant for a customer via the Partner Center CSP API — collect company details, place the customer record, capture the one-time admin password, order subscriptions (Business Premium, add-ons), and confirm tenant is live and accessible.

prerequisites: **Azure CLI** — authenticated to the partner tenant
**GDAP relationship** — must be approved by the customer before `tenant-configure` and later commands
**Graph API permissions**:
**Partner Center access** — Requires `Admin Agent` role in the CSP partner tenant

actions.name: msp-tenant-provisioning:tenant-azure-setup

prerequisites: **Add columns**: Computed columns, conditional columns, index columns
**Append queries**: Union multiple tables
**Azure CLI**: Required for authentication and Fabric REST API access.
**Change types**: Set correct data types
**Custom M**: Any custom Power Query M expressions
**Filter rows**: Remove nulls, filter by condition
**Group by**: Aggregate rows (sum, count, average, min, max)
**Merge queries**: Join with another table (inner, left outer, full outer)
**Node.js 18+**: Required for tooling and scripting.
**Pivot/unpivot**: Reshape data
**Power Query SDK** (optional): For local Dataflow Gen2 M query development.
**Rename columns**: Clean up column names
**Select/remove columns**: Keep only needed columns
Connection resources configured for source and sink systems.
Fabric workspace access with pipeline and dataflow author rights.
Workspace role: Admin, Member, or Contributor with item create permissions.

actions.description: Triage Fabric failures and degradations from Monitor Hub with repeatable severity and dependency analysis.

actions.name: notebook-pipeline-slo
actions.description: Define and validate notebook and pipeline SLOs with measurement logic, error budgets, and breach handling.

actions.name: observability-setup
actions.description: Set up Fabric observability with SLO definitions, signal inventory, and incident ownership.

prerequisites: Access to Monitor Hub, pipeline history, notebook run details, and workspace logs.
Alert routing channels with clear on-call ownership.
Defined service-level objectives for freshness, latency, and success rate.
Runbook location for incident response and postmortems.

fabric-onelake.description: Microsoft Fabric OneLake — unified data lake, shortcuts, file explorer, ADLS Gen2 APIs, and cross-workspace data access

actions.name: onelake-access-audit
actions.description: "Audit OneLake access roles, item permissions, sharing links, and workspace identity configuration"

actions.name: onelake-browse
actions.description: "Browse OneLake hierarchy — list workspaces, items, folders, and files via DFS API"

actions.name: onelake-file-api
actions.description: "Generate OneLake REST API or SDK code for file and directory operations"

actions.name: onelake-local-browse
actions.description: "Browse locally synced OneLake files — workspaces, lakehouses, tables, and files (no API auth needed)"

actions.name: onelake-setup
actions.description: Set up the Fabric OneLake plugin — install Azure CLI, authenticate, configure workspace access and OneLake endpoints

actions.name: onelake-upload
actions.description: "Upload local files or directories to a OneLake lakehouse via DFS API"

actions.name: shortcut-create
actions.description: "Create a OneLake shortcut to ADLS Gen2, Amazon S3, Google Cloud Storage, Dataverse, or another OneLake item"

prerequisites: **Node.js 18+**: Required for Azure SDK packages.
**Python 3.10+** (optional): Required for `azure-storage-file-datalake` Python SDK.
**npm**: Comes with Node.js.
Inconsistent permissions between related items (e.g., gold lakehouse more open than silver)
Items shared externally (B2B guests) without business justification
Items shared with `ReadAll` permission to broad audiences (grants access to all data)
Python: `DefaultAzureCredential` from `azure.identity`
TypeScript: `DefaultAzureCredential` from `@azure/identity`
curl: `az account get-access-token` for bearer token

powerbi-fabric.description: DAX measures, Power Query M, Power BI Embedded, deployment pipelines, PBIP scaffolding, Fabric Lakehouse, Direct Lake, performance optimization

actions.name: pbi-dashboard-create
actions.description: Create Power BI dashboards in Fabric with deterministic setup and verification checks.

actions.name: pbi-dataflow
actions.description: Scaffold a Fabric Dataflow Gen2 Power Query M definition for common source types with publishing guidance

actions.name: pbi-dataset-refresh
actions.description: Generate TypeScript code to trigger a Power BI dataset refresh and poll for completion status via the REST API.

actions.name: pbi-direct-lake-model
actions.description: Generate TMDL for a Direct Lake semantic model over a Fabric Lakehouse

actions.name: pbi-fabric-notebook
actions.description: Generate a Microsoft Fabric notebook (PySpark) for data transformation with Lakehouse integration. Outputs Python cells for reading, transforming, and writing Delta tables.

actions.name: pbi-fabric-pipeline
actions.description: Scaffold a Fabric Data Pipeline JSON definition with Copy and Notebook activities

actions.name: pbi-measure
actions.description: Generate a DAX measure from a natural language description. Outputs in the standard header comment format with optional TMDL output for PBIP projects.

actions.name: pbi-model-validate
actions.description: Validate a PBIP project directory — check TMDL syntax, lineage tags, measure references, and git readiness

actions.name: pbi-query
actions.description: Generate Power Query M code for data transformation or source connection. Supports multiple data source types and common transformation patterns.

actions.name: pbi-report-create
actions.description: Create Power BI reports in Fabric with deterministic validation and dependency checks.

actions.name: pbi-rls-role
actions.description: Generate Row-Level Security role definition — TMDL syntax and REST API payload, with static filter and dynamic USERPRINCIPALNAME() pattern

actions.name: pbi-scaffold
actions.description: Generate a complete PBIP (Power BI Project) structure with tables, measures, relationships, and report layout. Outputs files ready for Git.

actions.name: pbi-scorecard-manage
actions.description: Manage Power BI scorecards in Fabric with deterministic governance and verification checks.

actions.name: pbi-setup
actions.description: Set up the Power BI / Fabric plugin — configure Azure auth, verify workspace access, and optionally set up Fabric notebook environment

actions.name: pbi-workspace-create
actions.description: Generate TypeScript code to create and configure a Power BI workspace using the REST API, including user role assignments and capacity configuration.

prerequisites: API access configured for Power BI REST operations.
Permission to create and manage reports, dashboards, and scorecards in target workspaces.
Power BI tenant with Fabric enabled and workspace access.
Use MSAL `ConfidentialClientApplication` to acquire token for `https://analysis.windows.net/powerbi/api/.default`

powerbi-paginated-reports.description: Power BI paginated reports through Fabric — RDL authoring, VB.NET expressions, data source configuration, rendering/export, REST API automation, SSRS-to-Fabric migration, performance tuning, and troubleshooting

actions.name: pr-datasource
actions.description: Generate or update data source and dataset configuration for a paginated report. Supports Fabric Lakehouse, Warehouse, Semantic Model, Azure SQL, and Dataverse.

actions.name: pr-deploy
actions.description: Deploy a paginated report (.rdl) to a Fabric workspace via REST API. Generates TypeScript deployment script.

actions.name: pr-expression
actions.description: Generate VB.NET expressions for paginated reports from natural language descriptions. Supports formatting, conditionals, aggregates, lookups, and custom code.

actions.name: pr-migrate
actions.description: Analyze SSRS reports for Fabric compatibility and generate a migration plan with automated fixes.

actions.name: pr-scaffold
actions.description: Generate a complete RDL report template from a natural language description. Supports invoice, tabular, matrix, and subreport layouts.

actions.name: pr-setup
actions.description: Interactive setup for paginated report development — install Report Builder, configure Fabric workspace connection, verify prerequisites.

actions.name: pr-subscription
actions.description: Create or manage email subscriptions for paginated reports in the Power BI service via REST API.

actions.name: pr-validate
actions.description: Analyze an RDL file for common issues, deprecated features, Fabric compatibility problems, and best practice violations.

prerequisites: "Will you automate report deployment and export via REST API?"
**Delegated user** — For interactive tools (device code flow or auth code flow)
**Service principal** — For automated pipelines (app registration + client secret/certificate)
.NET Framework 4.7.2+ installed
Internet connectivity to Fabric/Power BI service
Windows OS (Report Builder is Windows-only)

process-task-mining.description: Process and task mining from M365, Power Automate, and Azure Monitor logs — extract event logs, discover process models, analyze performance, and check conformance

actions.name: conformance-check
actions.description: Compare actual event log against a reference process specification and produce a conformance report with fitness score and deviation details.

actions.name: log-extract
actions.description: Pull raw events from one or more Microsoft log sources and normalize to a unified event log CSV aligned with the PA Process Mining ingestion format.

actions.name: mining-setup
actions.description: Interactive multi-stage setup wizard — capture log source configuration, environment IDs, time window, auth requirements, and produce a mining-context.json for all subsequent commands.

actions.name: performance-analyze
actions.description: Analyze throughput time, waiting time, bottlenecks, and rework in an event log CSV. Produces a structured performance report with Mermaid diagram.

actions.name: process-discover
actions.description: From a unified event log CSV, construct a Directly-Follows Graph (DFG) and process variants table to discover what process actually runs.

actions.name: resource-analyze
actions.description: People and org mining — workload distribution per user, handover network, per-user conformance, and role/authorization audit.

azure-key-vault.description: Azure Key Vault — secrets, keys, and certificates management with RBAC, rotation policies, and managed identity integration

actions.name: kv-access-audit
actions.description: "Audit Key Vault RBAC assignments, review network rules, and check access logs"

actions.name: kv-app-integration
actions.description: "Configure App Service, Functions, or Container Apps to use Key Vault secrets via managed identity"

actions.name: kv-certificate-manage
actions.description: "Create self-signed or CA-integrated certificates, configure auto-renewal, and export"

actions.name: kv-rotation-policy
actions.description: "Set up automatic secret and key rotation with Event Grid notifications"

actions.name: kv-secret-manage
actions.description: "Create, read, update, list, and delete secrets in Azure Key Vault"

actions.name: kv-setup
actions.description: Set up the Azure Key Vault plugin -- install Azure CLI, create a Key Vault, configure RBAC access

prerequisites: **Azure CLI 2.50+**: Required for Key Vault management.
**Node.js 18+**: Required if using the Azure SDK for JavaScript.

entra-id-admin.description: Microsoft Entra ID administration via Graph API — full user/group lifecycle, directory roles, PIM, authentication methods, admin units, B2B guest management, license assignment, named locations, and entitlement management

actions.name: entra-access-package-create
actions.description: Create an entitlement management access package with resources, approval policy, and optional recurring access review

actions.name: entra-access-review-create
actions.description: Create a recurring access review for group membership, directory roles, or access package assignments

actions.name: entra-admin-setup
actions.description: Configure authentication and verify permissions for Entra ID administration

actions.name: entra-admin-unit-add
actions.description: Add a member (user/group) to an admin unit, or assign a scoped admin role within an admin unit

actions.name: entra-admin-unit-create
actions.description: Create an administrative unit for scoped delegation in Entra ID

actions.name: entra-auth-methods-get
actions.description: List registered authentication methods for a user — MFA methods, FIDO2 keys, TAPs, and registration status

actions.name: entra-auth-mfa-require
actions.description: Enforce MFA registration for users — per-user, by group, or tenant-wide report of unregistered users

actions.name: entra-collab-settings
actions.description: View and configure external collaboration settings — who can invite guests, guest permissions, and cross-tenant access

actions.name: entra-group-create
actions.description: Create a Microsoft 365 Group, Security Group, or Dynamic Security Group in Entra ID

actions.name: entra-group-list
actions.description: List and filter Entra ID groups by type, name pattern, owner, or member

actions.name: entra-group-member-add
actions.description: Add one or more users (or groups/service principals) to an Entra ID group

actions.name: entra-group-member-remove
actions.description: Remove one or more members from an Entra ID group

actions.name: entra-group-update
actions.description: Update group properties — display name, description, visibility, dynamic membership rule, or add/remove owners

actions.name: entra-guest-invite
actions.description: Invite an external guest user to your Entra ID tenant via B2B invitation

actions.name: entra-license-assign
actions.description: Assign or remove Microsoft 365 licenses from a user or group, with service plan control

actions.name: entra-license-report
actions.description: Generate a license usage report — available vs consumed, unassigned users, license errors, and expiring subscriptions

actions.description: Create an IP-based or country-based named location for use in Conditional Access policies

actions.name: entra-pim-assign
actions.description: Create a PIM eligible or active role assignment with duration and justification

actions.description: List PIM role assignments — eligible, active, and pending activations across the tenant or for a specific user

actions.name: entra-role-assign
actions.description: Assign a directory role to a user, group, or service principal — optionally scoped to an admin unit

actions.name: entra-role-list
actions.description: List all role assignments, show who has which roles, or list all roles available in the tenant

actions.name: entra-role-remove
actions.description: Remove a directory role assignment from a user, group, or service principal

actions.name: entra-user-bulk-import
actions.description: Bulk create Entra ID users from a CSV file using Graph batch API with error reporting

actions.name: entra-user-disable
actions.description: Disable or re-enable an Entra ID user account, revoke sessions, and optionally remove licenses

actions.name: entra-user-get
actions.description: Get comprehensive details for a user including licenses, group memberships, MFA status, and last sign-in

actions.name: entra-user-password-reset
actions.description: Reset an Entra ID user password, revoke sessions, and optionally force re-registration of MFA

graph-investigator.description: Microsoft Graph Investigator — unified user investigation, mailbox forensics, activity timelines, device correlation, and forensic reporting across all M365 services

actions.name: inv-apps
actions.description: OAuth consent and app permission audit — delegated grants, app role assignments, risky consent patterns, revocation

actions.name: inv-audit
actions.description: Search unified audit logs for a user — Exchange, SharePoint, Teams, Azure AD activities

actions.name: inv-devices
actions.description: Device inventory and activity for a user — Intune managed devices, Entra registered devices, compliance status, last sync

actions.name: inv-email
actions.description: Search Exchange mailbox messages — by sender, recipient, subject, date range, or keywords

actions.name: inv-files
actions.description: SharePoint and OneDrive file access investigation — accessed, downloaded, shared, deleted files; external sharing detection

actions.name: inv-risk
actions.description: Risk assessment and compromise indicator analysis — risk detections, impossible travel, mailbox rule changes, automated compromise score

actions.name: inv-setup
actions.description: Verify Microsoft Graph investigation permissions and test connectivity

actions.name: inv-signin
actions.description: Sign-in log analysis — location anomalies, IP analysis, failed logins, conditional access evaluation, risk levels

actions.name: inv-teams
actions.description: Teams chat and meeting activity investigation — chats, channel activity, meeting participation, call records

actions.name: inv-timeline
actions.description: Build unified cross-service activity timeline — combines sign-ins, audit logs, mailbox events, file access, Teams activity

actions.name: inv-user
actions.description: Comprehensive user profile investigation — groups, roles, licenses, auth methods, devices, recent sign-ins

prerequisites: `#microsoft.graph.emailAuthenticationMethod` — email OTP
`#microsoft.graph.fido2AuthenticationMethod` — hardware security key
`#microsoft.graph.microsoftAuthenticatorAuthenticationMethod` — Authenticator app
`#microsoft.graph.passwordAuthenticationMethod` — password is registered (always present)
`#microsoft.graph.phoneAuthenticationMethod` — SMS or voice call
`#microsoft.graph.windowsHelloForBusinessAuthenticationMethod` — Windows Hello

lighthouse-operations.description: Comprehensive Azure Lighthouse and M365 Lighthouse operations for MSPs/CSPs — Azure delegation ARM/Bicep templates, managed services marketplace offers, GDAP full lifecycle management, baseline deployment automation, cross-tenant governance, Partner Center integration, and alert management.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:azure-lighthouse-delegate
actions.description: Generate ARM/Bicep templates for Azure Lighthouse delegation, configure authorization tiers (permanent + JIT eligible), deploy to customer subscriptions or resource groups, verify delegation, and optionally set up cross-tenant Azure Policy assignments.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:baseline-deploy
actions.description: Scan M365 Lighthouse management template compliance across all managed tenants, identify non-compliant tenants, generate remediation plans with GDAP role requirements, and apply supported management actions via the Lighthouse API.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:gdap-manage
actions.description: Full GDAP relationship lifecycle management — create new relationships with approval links, assign security group roles, list expiring relationships, renew expiring ones, and terminate obsolete relationships with audit trail.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:lighthouse-alerts
actions.description: Manage M365 Lighthouse alerts across all managed tenants — list and triage active alerts by severity, acknowledge or dismiss alerts, generate remediation actions for risky users and malware, and configure alert notification rules.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:lighthouse-report
actions.description: Generate a comprehensive cross-tenant status report across all M365 Lighthouse managed tenants — MFA coverage, device compliance, risky users, GDAP expiry, baseline deployment, and Azure delegation health — formatted for MSP customer reviews or internal QBRs.

actions.name: lighthouse-operations:lighthouse-setup
actions.description: Configure partner tenant app registration for M365 Lighthouse access, verify GDAP connectivity, test Lighthouse API endpoints, and generate a readiness report showing which managed tenants are onboarded and accessible.

prerequisites: Azure CLI (`az`) — authenticated to the **partner tenant**
Microsoft Graph access — the signed-in identity needs:
Partner Center access (for CSP onboarding flow) — requires `Partner Center Admin Agent` role

sharing-auditor.description: SharePoint and OneDrive external sharing auditor — find overshared links, anonymous access, stale guest users, and auto-generate approval tasks for safe revocation

actions.name: sharing-remediate
actions.description: Generate approval-based revocation tasks for overshared links and stale guest users — safe remediation without immediate hard deletes.

actions.name: sharing-scan
actions.description: Scan SharePoint and OneDrive for overshared links, anonymous access, and stale guest users. Produces a risk-ranked sharing audit report.

actions.name: sharing-setup
actions.description: Set up the Sharing Auditor plugin — configure SharePoint admin access and Graph API permissions for sharing analysis

prerequisites: Graph API access with `Sites.Read.All`, `User.Read.All`
PnP PowerShell module (optional, for detailed sharing reports)
SharePoint Administrator role for site-level setting changes
`expirationDateTime` = `null` — link never expires
`hasPassword` = `false` — no password protection
`link.scope` = `anonymous` — anyone can access without sign-in
`roles` includes `write` or `owner` — edit access to external parties

actions.name: ado-workitem-create
actions.description: Create a work item (User Story, Bug, Task, Feature, Epic) in Azure DevOps with relations, tags, and attachments

prerequisites: Appropriate credentials for the target service
Authenticated to Azure DevOps (run `/ado-setup` first)
Azure DevOps Services or Server 2019+ with Analytics extension
Azure DevOps organization exists with at least one project
Azure DevOps organization with at least one repository
Azure DevOps project with a Git repository
Azure Key Vault access (if linking to Key Vault)
Azure Test Plans license or Basic + Test Plans access level
Delivery Plans extension installed (built-in for Azure DevOps Services)
Git installed (`git --version`)
Inherited process model enabled (Azure DevOps Services default)
Node.js 18+ installed
Organization-level `Manage process` permission
Service connections configured for deployment targets (if using deploy stage)
Source branch pushed to remote
Team membership for capacity management
Test plan and suite created (run `/ado-test-plan` first)
`Administer build resource permissions` for variable groups
`Administer service connections` permission
`Contribute to pull requests` permission on the repository
`Create repository` permission on the target project
`Create wiki` and `Edit wiki` permissions
`Create work items` permission in the target project
`Edit policies` permission on the repository
`Edit project-level information` for creating iterations
`Edit subscriptions` permission
`Manage agent pools` permission (organization-level)
`Manage dashboards` permission
`Manage environments` permission
`Manage extensions` permission (organization-level)
`Manage permissions` at the appropriate scope
`Manage test plans` and `Manage test suites` permissions
`Manage test runs` permission
`Queue builds` permission on the pipeline
`View analytics` permission
`View builds` permission on the project
`View work items in this node` permission
`az` CLI installed (optional but recommended)

azure-devops-orchestrator.description: Intelligent orchestration for Azure DevOps — ship work items with Claude Code, triage backlogs, plan sprints, coordinate releases, monitor pipelines, and balance workloads across projects. Integrates with microsoft-teams-mcp and microsoft-outlook-mcp when installed.

prerequisites: Azure DevOps PAT or authentication configured (`az devops login` or `AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PAT`)
Git repository (required for `/azure-devops-orchestrator:ship`)
`az devops` CLI extension installed (`az extension add --name azure-devops`)
`azure-devops` plugin installed (provides the Azure DevOps API / CLI layer)
Azure DevOps PAT or authentication configured (`az devops login` or `AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PAT`)
Git repository (required for `/azure-devops-orchestrator:ship`)
`az devops` CLI extension installed (`az extension add --name azure-devops`)
`azure-devops` plugin installed (provides the Azure DevOps API / CLI layer)
Azure DevOps PAT or authentication configured (`az devops login` or `AZURE_DEVOPS_EXT_PAT`)
Git repository (required for `/azure-devops-orchestrator:ship`)
`az devops` CLI extension installed (`az extension add --name azure-devops`)
`azure-devops` plugin installed (provides the Azure DevOps API / CLI layer)

azure-dotnet-webapp.description: Scaffold and build ASP.NET Core Web API and Blazor apps on Azure — Minimal API, controllers, Microsoft.Identity.Web, EF Core, SignalR, OpenAPI, App Service deployment, and Graph API integration patterns.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:add-webapi-operation
actions.description: Add an ASP.NET Core endpoint or Blazor service call that wraps a Microsoft Graph operation — preset patterns for file inventory, duplicate detection, and metadata, or a custom operation. Wires GraphServiceClient and ResiliencePipeline DI if not already present.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:deploy-webapp
actions.description: Provision Azure App Service (Plan + Web App + App Insights + Key Vault) with Bicep, deploy the ASP.NET Core app, configure Managed Identity and Key Vault access, set up staging slots, and generate a GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:scaffold-api
actions.description: Scaffold a Minimal API endpoint group or MVC controller with CRUD operations, service interface, DTOs, and OpenAPI metadata — wires the service into Program.cs automatically.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:scaffold-blazor
actions.description: Scaffold a Blazor page component with the selected render mode, service call, auth guard, models, and NavMenu entry — works with Blazor Server, WASM, and Auto render modes.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:webapp-setup
actions.description: Interactive setup wizard for ASP.NET Core Web API or Blazor projects on Azure — selects project type, auth, database, and Graph integration; scaffolds the full project structure with compilable stubs; and verifies the build.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:add-webapi-operation
actions.description: Add an ASP.NET Core endpoint or Blazor service call that wraps a Microsoft Graph operation — preset patterns for file inventory, duplicate detection, and metadata, or a custom operation. Wires GraphServiceClient and ResiliencePipeline DI if not already present.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:deploy-webapp
actions.description: Provision Azure App Service (Plan + Web App + App Insights + Key Vault) with Bicep, deploy the ASP.NET Core app, configure Managed Identity and Key Vault access, set up staging slots, and generate a GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps CI/CD pipeline.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:scaffold-api
actions.description: Scaffold a Minimal API endpoint group or MVC controller with CRUD operations, service interface, DTOs, and OpenAPI metadata — wires the service into Program.cs automatically.

actions.name: azure-dotnet-webapp:scaffold-blazor
actions.description: Scaffold a Blazor page component with the selected render mode, service call, auth guard, models, and NavMenu entry — works with Blazor Server, WASM, and Auto render modes.

actions.description: Interactive setup wizard for ASP.NET Core Web API or Blazor projects on Azure — selects project type, auth, database, and Graph integration; scaffolds the full project structure with compilable stubs; and verifies the build.

azure-graph-dotnet.description: Scaffold and build Microsoft Graph C# / .NET solutions on Azure — Functions, Container Jobs, Azure Identity, Polly resilience, and SharePoint file intelligence implementations.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:add-graph-operation
actions.description: Add a specific Microsoft Graph operation to an existing C# project — generates a service class, interface, model records, and DI registration for delta scan, metadata PATCH, file moves, or custom Graph calls.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:deploy-azure
actions.description: Deploy a C# Azure Functions or Container Job project to Azure — provision infrastructure with Bicep, push image to ACR, assign Managed Identity Graph permissions, and generate or update GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:dotnet-setup
actions.description: Interactive setup wizard for C# / .NET Azure Graph projects — checks prerequisites, selects hosting model, configures Azure Identity, validates toolchain, and writes appsettings / local.settings files.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:scaffold-console
actions.description: Scaffold a .NET 8 console app / Azure Container Job for long-running batch Graph operations — generic host, BackgroundService worker, Dockerfile, ACA Job Bicep, and Managed Identity role assignment script.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:scaffold-function
actions.description: Scaffold a new Azure Function class for a specific Graph operation — timer, HTTP, or Durable trigger — with DI wiring, resilience pipeline, logging, and cancellation token support.

prerequisites: Azure CLI: `https://aka.ms/installazurecli`
Docker: `https://docs.docker.com/get-docker/`
Functions Core Tools: `npm i -g azure-functions-core-tools@4 --unsafe-perm true`

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:add-graph-operation
actions.description: Add a specific Microsoft Graph operation to an existing C# project — generates a service class, interface, model records, and DI registration for delta scan, metadata PATCH, file moves, or custom Graph calls.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:deploy-azure
actions.description: Deploy a C# Azure Functions or Container Job project to Azure — provision infrastructure with Bicep, push image to ACR, assign Managed Identity Graph permissions, and generate or update GitHub Actions or Azure DevOps CI/CD pipelines.

actions.name: azure-graph-dotnet:dotnet-setup
actions.description: Interactive setup wizard for C# / .NET Azure Graph projects — checks prerequisites, selects hosting model, configures Azure Identity, validates toolchain, and writes appsettings / local.settings files.

fabric-developer-runtime.description: Microsoft Fabric developer runtime operations - GraphQL API, environments, user data functions, and variable library governance.

actions.name: environment-manage
actions.description: Manage Fabric runtime environments through deterministic create, update, validation, and retire workflows.

actions.name: graphql-api-manage
actions.description: Manage Fabric GraphQL API lifecycle with deterministic validation, guarded mutations, and post-change verification.

actions.name: runtime-setup
actions.description: Baseline Fabric developer runtime context, permissions, and deterministic safety gates.

actions.name: user-data-function-manage
actions.description: Manage Fabric user data functions with deterministic packaging, deployment, versioning, and rollback checks.

actions.name: variable-library-manage
actions.description: Govern Fabric variable libraries with deterministic lifecycle controls, least-privilege validation, and redacted outputs.

prerequisites: Action intent is explicit: `create`, `update`, `validate`, or `retire`.
Caller has `Fabric Workspace Admin` (or equivalent runtime governance role).
Caller has environment administration rights for the target Fabric workspace.
Caller has function deployment permissions in the target Fabric workspace.
Caller has runtime API authoring rights in the target Fabric workspace.
Caller has variable library governance permissions in the target workspace.
Environment action is explicit and approved for the target runtime stage.
Function package source, runtime dependencies, and owner approval are available.
Integration context is present and valid per [`docs/integration-context.md`](../../docs/integration-context.md).
Integration context validated against [`docs/integration-context.md`](../../docs/integration-context.md).
Microsoft Fabric tenant access for the target workspace.
Required permissions baseline: `Fabric Workspace Admin` or equivalent runtime governance role.
Runtime and environment administration rights for target Fabric workspaces.
Target workspace and environment scope are explicitly identified before execution.
Variable classification rules (secret vs non-secret) are defined before change execution.

fabric-gitops-cicd.description: Microsoft Fabric GitOps CI/CD — workspace Git integration, deployment pipelines, artifact promotion, branch strategy, and release validation

actions.name: deployment-pipeline-release
actions.description: Execute Fabric deployment pipeline promotions with preflight checks, evidence capture, and post-release validation.

actions.name: gitops-setup
actions.description: Set up Fabric GitOps foundations: repository model, branch controls, promotion policy, and environment ownership.

actions.name: promotion-checklist
actions.description: Generate a production promotion checklist covering data readiness, dependency safety, and rollback controls.

actions.name: workspace-git-connect
actions.description: Connect Fabric workspaces to Git with deterministic branch mapping and conflict-safe synchronization.

prerequisites: Artifact ownership model across notebooks, pipelines, models, and reports.
Fabric workspaces for dev/test/prod with deployment pipeline access.
Git repository with protected branches and pull request workflow.
Service principal or user identity authorized for promotion automation.

fluent-ui-design.description: Microsoft Fluent 2 design system mastery — design tokens, color system, typography, layout, components, Teams theming, advanced UI patterns, Griffel styling, accessibility, responsive design, and Figma design kits

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:audit
actions.description: Audit a React project for Fluent 2 design system compliance — token usage, accessibility, theming, and component best practices.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:chart
actions.description: Generate a Fluent-themed chart component with proper data binding, theming, and accessibility.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:component
actions.description: Scaffold a new React component using Fluent UI v9 best practices — makeStyles, tokens, slots, accessibility, and TypeScript.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:form
actions.description: Generate a Fluent UI form with validation — supports Formik/Yup or React Hook Form/Zod.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:griffel-optimize
actions.description: Analyze and optimize Griffel styling in a project — identify performance issues, add AOT compilation, fix anti-patterns.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:layout
actions.description: Generate a responsive layout pattern using Fluent 2 grid system, breakpoints, and spacing tokens.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:migrate-v8
actions.description: Scan a project for Fluent UI v8 usage and generate a v9 migration plan with component mapping and priority ordering.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:nextjs-setup
actions.description: Set up Fluent UI React v9 in a Next.js project with proper SSR configuration, FluentProvider, and optional Griffel AOT.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:office-addin
actions.description: Set up Fluent UI in an Office Add-in project — task pane with FluentProvider, theme integration, and Office.js hooks.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:setup
actions.description: Set up a new or existing project with Fluent UI React v9, including FluentProvider, theme configuration, and Teams SDK integration.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:test
actions.description: Generate tests for Fluent UI components — render tests, accessibility audits, theme switching, and interaction testing.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:theme
actions.description: Generate a custom Fluent 2 theme with brand ramp from a primary color, including light, dark, and high-contrast variants.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:tokens
actions.description: Look up Fluent 2 design token values and usage — color, spacing, typography, shadow, motion, and border-radius tokens.

actions.name: fluent-ui-design:web-component
actions.description: Scaffold a Fluent Web Component integration — register components, set up design tokens, and generate framework-specific wrapper code.

marketplace-dev-tools.description: Research Microsoft APIs, scaffold new plugins, extend existing ones, and audit marketplace coverage

actions.name: audit-coverage
actions.description: Audit marketplace plugin coverage against known Microsoft 365 services

actions.name: extend-plugin
actions.description: Add new endpoints and commands to an existing plugin

actions.name: research-service
actions.description: Research a Microsoft service's Graph API — endpoints, permissions, and schemas

actions.name: scaffold-plugin
actions.description: Generate a complete Claude plugin from research output

prerequisites: **Application**: permissions for daemon/service context
**Delegated (personal Microsoft account)**: usually "Not supported"
**Delegated (work or school account)**: permissions for signed-in user context

microsoft-docs-mcp.description: Search and fetch official Microsoft documentation via the Learn MCP server.

powerplatform-alm.description: Power Platform ALM — environments, solution transport, CI/CD pipelines, PCF controls, and deployment automation

actions.name: alm-env-create
actions.description: Create and configure a Power Platform environment with specified settings (type, region, language, currency, security group).

actions.name: alm-env-list
actions.description: List Power Platform environments with status, URL, type, and capacity information.

actions.name: alm-envvar-set
actions.description: Set Power Platform environment variable values for a specific environment, or generate deployment settings files.

actions.name: alm-pcf-build
actions.description: Build a PCF control and optionally deploy it to a development environment or package it in a solution.

actions.name: alm-pcf-init
actions.description: Scaffold a new PCF (PowerApps Component Framework) control from template — field, dataset, or React-based.

actions.name: alm-pipeline-generate
actions.description: Generate CI/CD pipeline YAML for Azure DevOps or GitHub Actions to automate Power Platform solution deployment.

actions.name: alm-setup
actions.description: Set up the Power Platform ALM plugin — install PAC CLI, configure environments, authenticate, and optionally set up CI/CD pipelines and PCF development

actions.name: alm-solution-diff
actions.description: Compare two Power Platform solutions to identify added, removed, and modified components across environments or versions.

actions.name: alm-solution-export
actions.description: Export a Power Platform solution as managed or unmanaged zip, with optional version setting and solution checker validation.

actions.name: alm-solution-import
actions.description: Import a Power Platform solution with connection reference and environment variable mapping via deployment settings.

prerequisites: **.NET SDK 6+** — run `dotnet --version` and confirm the major version is >= 6
**Node.js 18+** — run `node --version` and confirm the major version is >= 18
**PowerShell 7+** — run `pwsh --version` and confirm the major version is >= 7

prerequisites: **.NET SDK 6+** — run `dotnet --version` and confirm the major version is >= 6
**Node.js 18+** — run `node --version` and confirm the major version is >= 18
**PowerShell 7+** — run `pwsh --version` and confirm the major version is >= 7

fabric-distribution-apps.description: Microsoft Fabric org app distribution - package, permission model, release, and adoption workflows for organizational app rollout.

actions.name: org-app-adoption-report
actions.description: Produce deterministic Fabric org app adoption reporting with rollout evidence, permission coverage, and risk-focused insights.

actions.name: org-app-package
actions.description: Build and validate Fabric org app package artifacts with deterministic versioning and integrity checks.

actions.name: org-app-permission-model
actions.description: Define and verify a least-privilege Fabric org app permission model with deterministic approval and validation flow.

actions.name: org-app-release
actions.description: Execute staged Fabric org app release with deterministic approval gates, rollout checks, and rollback readiness verification.

actions.name: org-app-setup
actions.description: Validate Fabric org app preview readiness, context, permissions, and release guardrails before distribution actions.

prerequisites: App metadata, dependency manifest, and release notes are prepared.
Approved package version and permission model are already in place.
Audience group inventory and owner approvals are available.
Caller has `Fabric Tenant Admin` or delegated org app publisher permissions.
Caller has org app packaging and publishing permissions.
Caller has read permissions for org app release telemetry and audience assignments.
Caller has release authority for target rollout stage.
Caller has tenant-level app permission governance rights.
Integration context is valid per [`docs/integration-context.md`](../../docs/integration-context.md).
Microsoft Fabric tenant access with organizational app distribution features enabled.
Reporting window and audience scope are explicit.
Required permissions baseline: `Fabric Tenant Admin` or delegated publisher governance role.
Target rollout scope (workspace, audience baseline, and channel) is explicitly defined.
Tenant/workspace permissions for app packaging, release, and audience assignment.

onenote-knowledge-base.description: OneNote Knowledge Base - headless-first Graph automation for advanced page architecture, styling, and task workflows

actions.name: onenote-bulk-style-rollout
actions.description: Roll out OneNote visual themes across sections or notebooks with dry-run and drift reporting

actions.name: onenote-columns-layout
actions.description: Build and maintain polished multi-column OneNote layouts using supported table-based XHTML patterns

actions.name: onenote-create-page
actions.description: Create polished OneNote pages with template-driven structure, style tokens, tags, and patch anchors

actions.name: onenote-hierarchy-manage
actions.description: Manage notebook architecture, nested section groups, and parent-child page organization patterns

actions.name: onenote-meeting-notes
actions.description: Create premium meeting pages with structured decisions, action tracking, and polished formatting

actions.name: onenote-navigation-index
actions.description: Build and maintain parent-child navigation indexes, backlinks, and cross-page relationship maps

actions.name: onenote-page-patch
actions.description: Apply deterministic OneNote page PATCH operations for incremental updates to anchored content blocks

actions.name: onenote-quality-audit
actions.description: Audit OneNote page quality for structure, styling, accessibility, patchability, and stale task risk

actions.name: onenote-search
actions.description: Advanced OneNote search across notebooks with scope control, tag slicing, and recency ranking

actions.name: onenote-setup
actions.description: Headless-first OneNote setup with non-interactive auth, permission checks, and readiness validation

actions.name: onenote-style-apply
actions.description: Apply and normalize OneNote visual themes for fonts, headers, colors, callouts, and status chips

actions.name: onenote-task-tracker
actions.description: Create and maintain OneNote task and action boards with tags, owner routing, and due-date governance

actions.name: onenote-template-library
actions.description: Manage reusable OneNote page templates for SOPs, decisions, runbooks, and meeting workflows

prerequisites: `AZURE_CLIENT_ID`
`AZURE_CLIENT_SECRET` (or certificate-based equivalent)
`AZURE_TENANT_ID`

planner-orchestrator.description: Intelligent orchestration for Microsoft Planner — ship tasks with Claude Code, triage backlogs, plan sprint buckets, monitor deadlines, and balance workloads across plans. Integrates with microsoft-teams-mcp, microsoft-outlook-mcp, and powerbi-fabric when installed.

prerequisites: Git repository (required for `/planner-orchestrator:ship`)
Microsoft Graph delegated permissions: `Tasks.ReadWrite`, `Group.Read.All`
`planner-todo` plugin installed (provides the Graph API layer)
Git repository (required for `/planner-orchestrator:ship`)
Microsoft Graph delegated permissions: `Tasks.ReadWrite`, `Group.Read.All`
`planner-todo` plugin installed (provides the Graph API layer)
Git repository (required for `/planner-orchestrator:ship`)
Microsoft Graph delegated permissions: `Tasks.ReadWrite`, `Group.Read.All`
`planner-todo` plugin installed (provides the Graph API layer)

planner-todo.description: Microsoft Planner and To Do task management via Graph API — classic plans, Premium Dataverse projects, buckets, tasks, assignments, checklists, nested plans, roster plans, sprints, goals, and Business Scenarios

actions.name: planner-bucket-create
actions.description: Create a new bucket in a Planner plan

actions.name: planner-business-scenario
actions.description: Create and manage Business Scenario isolated task containers — app-owned Planner tasks with access control policies

actions.name: planner-goals
actions.description: Create and manage goals and OKRs in a Planner Premium project via Dataverse

actions.name: planner-nested-plan
actions.description: Create a Planner plan nested inside a task, enabling hierarchical plan decomposition

actions.name: planner-plan-create
actions.description: Create a new Planner plan with buckets for a Microsoft 365 Group

actions.name: planner-roster-create
actions.description: Create a roster-based Planner plan that does not require a Microsoft 365 Group

actions.name: planner-setup
actions.description: Set up the Planner & To Do plugin — configure Azure auth and verify Graph API access to plans and task lists

actions.name: planner-sprint
actions.description: Create, list, and manage sprints/iterations in a Planner Premium project via Dataverse

actions.name: planner-task-assign
actions.description: Assign or reassign a Planner task to one or more users

actions.name: planner-task-completion
actions.description: Set task completion requirements — require all checklist items checked, a form submitted, or approval before marking complete

actions.name: planner-task-create
actions.description: Create a task in a Planner plan with optional assignment and due date

actions.name: planner-task-dependency
actions.description: Add or remove predecessor/successor task dependencies in a Planner Premium project (requires Microsoft Project license and Dataverse access)

actions.name: planner-task-details
actions.description: Update task details — add checklist items, reference links, and description

actions.name: planner-task-list
actions.description: List and filter tasks in a Planner plan — by assignee, due date range, or completion status

actions.name: planner-task-notes
actions.description: Update a Planner task's rich HTML notes and description (replaces plain-text description)

actions.name: planner-task-update
actions.description: Update a Planner task — change progress, move to different bucket, set priority, or mark complete

actions.name: todo-list-create
actions.description: Create a new Microsoft To Do task list

actions.name: todo-task-create-recurring
actions.description: Create a recurring Microsoft To Do task with a daily, weekly, monthly, or yearly recurrence pattern

actions.name: todo-task-create
actions.description: Create a task in a Microsoft To Do list with optional due date and reminder

actions.name: todo-tasks-list
actions.description: List Microsoft To Do tasks from one or all task lists, with optional filters

prerequisites: Application permission requires admin consent
User must hold a **Microsoft Project** license
`BusinessScenarioData.ReadWrite.All` — for creating and managing scenario tasks
`Group.ReadWrite.All` — associate the plan with the specified M365 Group
`Tasks.ReadWrite`
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage Planner buckets
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage Planner plans and buckets
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage Planner tasks
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage To Do tasks including recurring ones
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage the signed-in user's To Do lists and tasks
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — create and manage the signed-in user's To Do tasks
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — for reading Planner tasks
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — read and modify Planner task assignments
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — read and update Planner task details
`Tasks.ReadWrite` — read and update Planner tasks
`Tasks.Read` — read Planner tasks (use `Tasks.ReadWrite` if you also need to modify)
`Tasks.Read` — read the signed-in user's To Do tasks and lists
`https://<org>.crm.dynamics.com/.default` — Dataverse delegated scope

power-automate.description: Design and troubleshoot Power Automate cloud flows — trigger/action patterns, run diagnostics, retries, and deployment-safe flow definitions

actions.name: flow-debug
actions.description: Diagnose failed Power Automate runs with evidence-driven root-cause hypotheses, validation steps, and safe remediations.

actions.name: flow-deploy-check
actions.description: Run a deployment readiness review for a Power Automate flow, including connection references, variables, ownership, and rollback posture.

actions.name: flow-design
actions.description: Design a maintainable Power Automate cloud flow with trigger, actions, control paths, retries, and idempotency guidance.

actions.name: flow-setup
actions.description: Prepare Power Automate flow work by confirming environment boundaries, connectors, scale expectations, and failure handling.

actions.name: pa-ai-builder
actions.description: Design and integrate an AI Builder model into a Power Automate flow — model selection, training guidance, flow integration patterns, confidence thresholds, and human review routing.

actions.name: pa-bpf-design
actions.description: Design a Business Process Flow (BPF) for a Dataverse entity — stages, steps, branching logic, required fields, cloud flow triggers at stage transitions, and solution packaging.

actions.name: pa-custom-connector
actions.description: Design and build a custom Power Platform connector from an existing REST API — OpenAPI 2.0 spec, authentication configuration, code policy, webhook triggers, and solution packaging.

actions.name: pa-governance
actions.description: Audit and remediate Power Platform governance issues — DLP policies, orphaned flows, inactive automations, license compliance, environment health, and CoE Toolkit reports.

prerequisites: **API Key**: define `in: header` or `in: query`, set `name`
**Basic**: simple `type: basic` securityDefinition
**Client credentials**: `flow: application` with `tokenUrl` and tenant-specific scope
**OAuth2 authorization code**: include `authorizationUrl`, `tokenUrl`, `refreshUrl`, `scopes`
For service accounts running cloud flows: list required privileges
Identify roles that need `prvReadProcessSession` + `prvWriteProcessSession`
Note any client ID/secret placeholder values — document where to enter them
Security roles to assign (system-generated roles vs custom)

servicedesk-runbooks.description: M365 service desk auto-runbooks — guided workflows for common tickets like shared mailbox access, MFA reset, file recovery, and password reset with pre-checks, approval gates, and end-user verification

actions.name: runbook-password-reset
actions.description: Reset a user's password — guided workflow with compliance checks, secure password generation, and delivery instructions.

actions.name: runbook-recover-file
actions.description: Recover a deleted file from OneDrive or SharePoint recycle bin — guided search, preview, and restore.

actions.name: runbook-reset-mfa
actions.description: Reset MFA for a user — guided workflow with identity verification, method removal, and re-registration instructions.

actions.name: runbook-shared-mailbox
actions.description: Grant shared mailbox access — guided workflow with pre-checks, permission type selection, and end-user notification.

actions.name: servicedesk-setup
actions.description: Set up the Service Desk Runbooks plugin — configure Graph and Exchange access for common ticket workflows

prerequisites: "Can read all emails in the mailbox" → Full Access
"Can send emails on behalf of the mailbox (shows 'on behalf of')" → Send on Behalf
"Can send emails that appear to come from the mailbox" → Send As
Exchange Online Management module for mailbox operations
Graph API access with `User.ReadWrite.All`, `UserAuthenticationMethod.ReadWrite.All`
SharePoint access for file recovery runbooks

sharepoint-file-intelligence.description: Scan, categorize, deduplicate, and organize SharePoint and OneDrive files at scale using Microsoft Graph.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:apply-categories
actions.description: Apply metadata columns and content-type tags to SharePoint files based on pattern-matching rules. Supports dry-run preview before committing batch PATCH operations.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:consolidate-files
actions.description: Move SharePoint files to target folders based on a YAML move-mapping file. Defaults to dry-run preview, requires user confirmation, executes Graph API moves in batches, and generates a rollback script.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:find-duplicates
actions.description: Find exact and near-duplicate files in a SharePoint inventory. Groups duplicates by hash, name+size, and version patterns, then reports potential space savings.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:scan-inventory
actions.description: Enumerate all files in a SharePoint site or OneDrive drive and produce a CSV or JSON inventory report with file metadata, sizes, owners, and types.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:sfi-setup
actions.description: Full interactive setup for the SharePoint File Intelligence plugin — scope wizard, auth mode selection, app registration walkthrough, connectivity tests, probe scan, settings file generation, and starter category rules.

prerequisites: **App registration (service principal)** — Recommended for automated/background scans. Requires Azure admin to register an app and grant consent.
**Delegated (device code flow)** — Easiest to start. Authenticates as your user account. Good for one-off scans but tokens expire.
**I already have an access token** — Paste a token directly into `MICROSOFT_ACCESS_TOKEN`. Useful for testing or CI pipelines.

actions.name: sharepoint-file-intelligence:sfi-setup
actions.description: Full interactive setup for the SharePoint File Intelligence plugin — scope wizard, auth mode selection, app registration walkthrough, connectivity tests, probe scan, settings file generation, and starter category rules.

prerequisites: **App registration (service principal)** — Recommended for automated/background scans. Requires Azure admin to register an app and grant consent.
**Delegated (device code flow)** — Easiest to start. Authenticates as your user account. Good for one-off scans but tokens expire.
**I already have an access token** — Paste a token directly into `MICROSOFT_ACCESS_TOKEN`. Useful for testing or CI pipelines.

teams-lifecycle.description: Teams lifecycle management — create and archive teams with templates, enforce naming and ownership, apply sensitivity labels, and run expiration reviews using non-technical 'project start/end' language

actions.name: teams-lifecycle-setup
actions.description: Set up the Teams Lifecycle Manager — configure Teams admin access, templates, and naming policies

actions.name: teams-project-end
actions.description: End a project — archive a team with content preservation, owner notification, and expiration review. Non-technical guided wizard.

actions.name: teams-project-start
actions.description: Start a new project — create a team from a template with proper naming, ownership, and sensitivity label. Non-technical guided wizard.

actions.name: teams-review
actions.description: Audit teams for governance compliance — check ownership, activity, naming, sensitivity labels, and expiration status.

prerequisites: Graph API access with `Team.Create`, `Group.ReadWrite.All`, `Reports.Read.All`
Teams Administrator role for governance settings
